titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Abraham Ravett Brighton Beach photographs 2012.009
titlestmt.author: Dee Bowers

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-19 20:11:08 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.abstract: 192 large format black and white photographic negatives and corresponding work prints by Abraham Ravett of Brighton Beach scenes and people from summer 1982 and March 1984, as well as two shot notebooks and one book of the photographer's work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The photographer numbered each negative and its correponding work print and arranged them in numerical order, which is also roughly chronological with some exceptions. The collection has retained the photographer's original order.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: There is an item level listing of the images in the collection in its accession file, available to researchers upon request.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Abraham Ravett Brighton Beach photographs, 2012.009, photograph number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was originally stored in a small metal chest. The archivist rehoused the materials in acid-free folders and boxes but maintained the photographer's original order. The metal chest was discarded.

Also included was a piece of card with shot notes on pieces of masking tape attached. The adhesive on the tape was deteriorating and the pieces were coming loose. The archivist photocopied the item for preservation and discarded the original.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Ravett, Abraham. Brighton Beach : photographs. 2013. Call number TR659.8.R38 B7 2013
Series 7: Brighton Beach in the Brooklyn location scouting photographs collection (CBHM.0017)
George Cohen photographs (accession 2013.0007) - available on CBH's digital collections under the call number prefix COHEN.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 192 large format photographic negatives and corresponding work prints by Abraham Ravett of Brighton Beach scenes and people from summer 1982 and March 1984. Two handwritten shot list notebooks in chronological order from June (blue) and July (green) 1982. One 2013 book "Brighton Beach: Photographs by Abraham Ravett" with images from these negatives.
The negatives are black and white, 8x10", shot with a Wista view camera. The work prints are black and white and approximately 8x10 (some are slightly larger).

Subjects include neighborhood residents of all ages, but especially senior citizens, including Ravett's mother; the beach and boardwalk; local businesses; street scenes, including the elevated subway tracks that run through the neighborhood; parks and playgrounds; homes and architecture; and the housing development Trump Village.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: There is an additional copy of this book shelved in the CBH library under the call number TR659.8.R38 B7 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes a preservation photocopy of a piece of board with shot notes on pieces of masking tape.

did.unittitle: Negatives and work prints BB1 - BB80

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Named individuals in these photographs include Mrs. Bloom, Mr. and Mrs. Wolfish, Esther Friedenberg, Jack Harris, Mr. Noodelman, Mr. Moss, and Mr. Nadler.
Identified locations include Trump Village, Club 28, Ocean Parkway, Brighton Street, the boardwalk, and the alleyway between Brighton 7th and 8th Streets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Named individuals in these images include David Lam, Lawrence Cohen, Vincent Commiss, Mr. Leonard, Mr. and Mrs. Baver, Mr. Newman, Evelyn Reiss, Mr. and Mrs. Wolfish, Esther Friedenberg, Jack Harris, Morris Schindelheim, Rebecca Gormazano, and Mr. and Mrs. G. Segal.
Identified locations include Coney Island Avenue, Marine Apartments, Brighton Beach Avenue, Trump Village, Brighton 7th Street, Youth World, Ocean Parkway, the boardwalk, and Neptune Avenue.
